description: Aromas of herbs and desert brush blend with raisiny berry scents. This feels grabby and tannic, while jammy, herbal berry flavors flatten out with airing. A loamy tasting finish loses some intensity and spirit as this settles.
title: Salentein 2014 Reserve Malbec (Valle de Uco)

description: A soft and indistinct wine that is fat and has only the merest hint of fruit. It is round with some fresh acidity at the end.
title: Quinta do Cavalinho 2014 Vale das Donas White (Tejo)

description: Briny cherry and red-currant aromas are light. This feels sharp on the palate, with fresh flavors of red plum, tomato and a salty hint.
title: Millaman 2014 Estate Reserve Pinot Noir (Leyda Valley)

description: Aromas seem blunted, but the palate shows both freshness and fruit. This is balanced and comes out with surprisingly friendly apple fruit, finishing on a fresh note.
title: Domaine Moltès 2014 Tradition Pinot Gris (Alsace)

description: Ripe-tasting spiced-plum flavors and a generous texture make this full-bodied wine easy to enjoy at first sip, and it offers even more payoff with continued tasting. It delivers plenty of fruit character, accented by complex cinnamon, clove and nutmeg spices and a grand feeling of breadth on the palate.
title: FEL 2014 Donnelly Creek Pinot Noir (Anderson Valley)

description: A lifted, phenolic note of Bosc pear peel suggests both freshness and maturity. The soft, rounded palate sings with pear sweetness and finishes medium-dry. There is softness and pliability, but a guiding, focused freshness that provides excellent balance.
title: Domaine Moltès 2014 Steinert Grand Cru Pinot Gris (Alsace)

description: Fermented via carbonic maceration in a concrete tank, this spent 10 months in neutral oak prior to bottling. Give it a bit of breathing time to flesh out, and it rewards you with lush, spicy berry flavors, accents of fresh herb, and a hint of chocolate.
title: Evening Land 2014 Seven Springs Estate Vielles Vignes Gamay (Eola-Amity Hills)

description: Domaine de la Mordorée's 2014 rosés are fitting tributes to proprietor Christophe Delorme's final vintage (he died in June 2015). This blend of 50% Grenache, 30% Syrah and 20% Cinsault is weighty and rich, blending raspberries and white chocolate into a wine that could be served as a light red wine, with grilled meats or poultry.
title: Domaine de la Mordorée 2014 Le Dame Rousse Rosé (Côtes du Rhône)

description: As you'd expect from this pioneering Oregon winery, this is a smooth, sophisticated, accessible yet ageable wine. Polished berry and cherry fruit is swathed in cola and cocoa highlights, running through silky tannins. Drink now through 2020.
title: Adelsheim 2014 Breaking Ground Pinot Noir (Chehalem Mountains)

description: Scattered and unruly up front, this smells downright foxy. The palate is both narrow and sharp, while flavors of vanilla oak, soupy black fruits and tart acidity come around a bit on the finish.
title: Trus 2014 Roble  (Ribera del Duero)

description: Winemaker Tyler Russell sources the fruit for this bottling from an extremely cool-climate vineyard. Tons of fresh-pressed boysenberries meet with wood ash, underripe berries, black olives, rosemary, roasted lamb and Mediterranean spices on the nose. Tart black-plum skin is layered with bay leaf, rosemary, thyme, crushed peppercorn and charred beef on the dynamic palate.
title: Cordant 2014 Presqu'ile Vineyard Syrah (Santa Maria Valley)

description: Rain on cement, lemon pith and the slightest hint of roasted nuts show on the rather tightly wound nose of this vineyard blend. The flavors are delicate and refreshing at first on the brisk palate, and then they warm up pleasantly into marcona almond and a bit of butter.
title: Cru 2014 Vineyard Montage Chardonnay (Monterey County)

description: A blend of 45% Cannonau, 45% Carignano and 10% Bovale Sardo, this opens with aromas of cassis, red cherry and Mediterranean scrub. The round juicy palate offers black plum, ripe raspberry and white pepper set against pliant tannins. Enjoy through 2019.
title: Vigne Surrau 2014 Surrau Red (Isola dei Nuraghi)

description: Aromatic and inviting, this pushes strawberry preserves, cola and a dash of non-carbonated seltzer into the midpalate. It spent 14 months in one-quarter new French oak, and the color along with the already maturing fruit flavors suggest drinking this now through 2020.
title: Paul O'Brien 2014 Pinot Noir (Umpqua Valley)

description: This fruity, friendly Pinot opens with ripe berry aromas mixed with touches of minerality and latex. It feels fresh and sturdy, with black cherry and raspberry flavors. A pure, sweet, lightly chocolaty finish wraps things up.
title: Ritual 2014 Pinot Noir (Casablanca Valley)

description: Fruity and almost off dry, this appealing wine goes heavy on flavors of spicy pear and apricot jam. A streak of honey runs through, and a hint of wintergreen. It's already drinking well, so dive in.
title: Raptor Ridge 2014 Pinot Gris (Willamette Valley)

description: This wine is 57% Cabernet Sauvignon, 22% Cabernet Franc, 13% Merlot, 5% Petit Verdot and 3% Malbec. Aromas of cocoa, bittersweet chocolate, wood spice, graphite and herb lead to sweet full-bodied plum and coffee flavors. It has a lot of textural appeal, with lightly chalky tannins providing support.
title: Spring Valley Vineyard 2014 Frederick Estate Grown Red (Walla Walla Valley (WA))

description: This definitely fits the bill as an affordable weekday red. Red berries, vanilla and spice on the nose and palate offer plenty of enjoyment, while there's just enough softly dusty tannins to provide structure on the crisp finish. Drink now through the end of 2018.
title: Oyster Bay 2014 Merlot (Hawke's Bay)

description: A rather subdued nose turns into a citric pool of grapefruit pulp and peel on the palate. This is fresh and easy, light and zippy. The finish is clean-cut and refreshing. Drink soon.
title: Gross 2014 Jakobi Sauvignon Blanc (Südsteiermark)

description: This is a small estate of 12 acres dominated by its 95% Merlot. It is ripe and juicy with spice, dried fruits and crisp black currants. The tannins are already well integrated with the fruitiness. Drink from late 2017.
title: Château Lamour 2014  Castillon Côtes de Bordeaux

description: Appealing aromas of black cherry, cocoa, herb, pencil lead and wood spice are followed by generous pit-fruit flavors. The tannins show some chalkiness and astringency.
title: North by Northwest 2014 Cabernet Sauvignon (Columbia Valley (WA))

description: This is a solid Verdejo with stony aromas of citrus fruits, grapefruit in particular. A palate with citric acidity creates a fresh platform for orange and grapefruit flavors. A round plump finish with briny citrus flavors is a good ending.
title: Marqués de Irún 2014 Verdejo (Rueda)

description: This dark-colored, extracted Priorat smells largely of oak and mint on top of lightly herbal, green-leaning plum and currant. The palate is wiry, with citric acidity that creates tartness to go with monotone red-fruit flavors. Rough, tough and crisp on the finish, with tomatoey acidity and hard tannins.
title: Portal del Priorat 2014 Gotes del Priorat Grenache-Carignan (Priorat)

description: Following a rather neutral nose, the freshness and texture of grapefruit peel fizzes away on the palate. There is concentration, briskness and texture while the finish is lip-smackingly lemony. Drink soon.
title: Leth 2014 Hofweingarten Roter Veltliner (Wagram)

description: Bold flavors and plenty of creamy texture make this full-bodied wine seem big and mouth-filling. It offers nice lemon and butter aromas, ripe pear and spicy nutmeg flavors along with a smoky overtone that lasts from the aroma to the finish.
title: Lava Cap 2014 Reserve Estate Bottled Chardonnay (El Dorado)

description: An earthy hint accompanies the scent of ripe pear on the nose. The palate is diffuse with fruity pear and fresh lemon resulting in a very refreshing, textured wine with some concentration. The finish is lasting and dry.
title: Dopff Au Moulin 2014 Schoenenbourg Grand Cru Pinot Gris (Alsace)

description: Dill, crushed herb, oak, toast and balsamic aromas emerge in the glass as this wine opens. Its firm palate offers dried cherry, licorice and espresso flavors framed by close-grained tannins and vibrant acidity. It has structure but lacks fruit richness.
title: La Spinetta 2014 Valeirano  (Barbaresco)

description: Partly wood aged, this wine is conceived as the essence of Malbec. With its concentrated black fruits and firm tannins, it is dense and needs to age. There is a core that is firmly structured—rich and still surrounded by youthful fruitiness. Drink from 2019.
title: Château Lamartine 2014 Prestige du Malbec (Cahors)

description: Soft and inviting on the plushly texture nose, this bottling by a Napa winery sourcing from the iconic Santa Maria vineyard offers popcorn, butterscotch, fresh apple blossom and salted stone-fruit aromas. The flavors are classic and straightforward in the citrusy style, with Meyer lemon, nectarine, pineapple and butter.
title: Castello di Amorosa 2014 Bien Nacido Vineyard Chardonnay (Santa Maria Valley)

description: With vines situated on the slopes above the Garonne River, this Terra Vitis bottling is rich and spicy, full of blackberry fruits and still young acidity. Sixty percent Merlot gives it its smooth character although it is the Cabernet Franc that adds its subtle perfumes. The wine will need time and should be ready to drink from 2020.
title: Château la Grange Clinet 2014 Grande Réserve  (Côtes de Bordeaux)

description: Crisp canteloupe rind and apple notes penetrate deeply on the palate of this dry, full-bodied Chardonnay. Whiffs of savory reeds and herbs distract a bit on the midpalate, but the lemon-lime finish is revitalizing and refreshing. Drink now–2017.
title: Billsboro 2014 Atwater Vineyards Chardonnay (Finger Lakes)

description: This wine all comes from Rosebud vineyard and is blended with 17% Sémillon. Bright aromas of lees, pineapple, fig and lemon are followed by tart, full-bodied flavors with mouthwatering acidity.
title: Elevation Cellars 2014 Sauvignon Blanc (Wahluke Slope)

description: Grapy aromas of black plum and blackberry are balsamic and a bit herbal. This blend of mostly Malbec and Syrah is medium in body and somewhat juicy. Blackberry, plum and peppery flavors finish chocolaty and lightly herbal.
title: Domaine Bousquet 2014 Gaia Red (Tupungato)

description: This lower-priced Pinot from the Center of Effort team punches way above its weight in this vintage, showing light cherry-blossom and black-cherry aromas mixed with gamy meat and wet slate. Blackberry pie is cut with orange rind on the sip, with an earthy grip showing flavors of juniper, crushed clove and pipe tobacco.
title: Fossil Point 2014 Pinot Noir (Edna Valley)

description: Named after a blue butterfly that is found in the vineyard, this wine, made from organically grown grapes, is bright, crisp and possibly too young. It has a really fresh, mineral character, very tangy with lemon zest and pink grapefruit. It will be even better in a few months, so drink from 2016.
title: Alliance Loire 2014 Azuré Sur Lie  (Muscadet Sèvre et Maine)

description: Wonderfully apple-scented, just like a freshly cut Cox Orange Pippin, this is dry Pinot Gris with extra verve from the 2014 vintage. Drink soon while its aromas are at their delicious peak.
title: Kuentz-Bas 2014 Collection Pinot Gris (Alsace)

description: There's plenty of nuance and detail on this concrete-aged wine, with its baked apple and spice aromas and flavors. It shows a fine sense of elegance and balance.
title: Columbia Crest 2014 Reserve Estate Unoaked Chardonnay (Columbia Valley (WA))

description: From vines averaging 60 years of age in Jurassic chalky clay, this ripe, buttery wine has plenty of ripe apples, pears and a generous texture. An attractive touch of toast adds to the complexity. Drink now.
title: Pierre Merguey 2014 Clos de la Maison  (Saint-Véran)

description: Named after a local aristocrat, this wine is soft, fruity and fresh. Red berry fruits are light and crisp. Behind them, gentle tannins give a hint of structure. The wine is ready to drink.
title: Marcolino Sebo 2014 Visconde de Borba Red (Alentejo)

description: This soft, broad-textured wine has low-intensity aromas and flavors. It tastes simple and smooth, medium bodied and on the edge of sweetness.
title: J Vineyards & Winery 2014 Pinot Gris (California)

description: This wine tastes heavy and rounded, with whiffs of petroleum and peppermint on the entry that settle down over time. With ripe fruit and oak at its core, it's a wine for fans of a more generous style.
title: ZD 2014 Chardonnay (California)

description: Punchy red fruit meets brawny tannin and substantial oak in this red, which includes small amounts of Syrah and Cabernet Sauvignon. Its cherry and cassis notes are seasoned with dried herb highlights.
title: Rutherford Hill 2014 Merlot (Napa Valley)

description: The top wine of the trilogy made at Château Ponzac, this is designed for aging. Mint flavors from new-wood barrels mingle with dark tannins and a concentrated texture. It is a powerful, ripe wine that certainly does need some time. Don't drink before 2020.
title: Château Ponzac 2014 Eternellement Malbec (Cahors)

description: Vines situated close to the fortified citadel of Blaye have produced this bright and shiny 2014. With its clear red-berry fruits, black currants and acidity, it is immediately attractive. The tannins, a great support to the fruit, promise some aging. Drink now, or, better yet, wait until 2018.
title: Château Haut Colombier 2014  Blaye Côtes de Bordeaux

description: Black plum, boysenberry, nutmeg and cinnamon show on the nose of this appellation blend. A ripe boysenberry flavor pops at the tip of the sip, with sagebrush and savory meat flavors arising midpalate. The tannic structure is squeaky and clean.
title: Cinnabar 2014 Pinot Noir (Santa Cruz Mountains)

description: This serious wine is spicy both from wood aging and from the natural character of the Cabernet Franc. It is rich, intense and with layers of red fruits balanced with a strongly smoky character. The wine has a tight texture, suggesting it needs further aging. Drink from 2019.
title: Château de Parnay 2014  Saumur-Champigny

description: While intensely ripe on the nose, this beautifully balanced Bordeaux-style blend offsets luscious fruit with dazzling acidity and a rigid frame of fine tannins. Black plum, cherry and currant flavors are succulent but pristine, penetrating deeply through a long finish. Drinks well now but should improve through 2021.
title: Martha Clara 2014 Northville Red (North Fork of Long Island)

description: Vanilla, cedar and plum notes benefit from a dusting of cracked pepper in this medium-bodied dry red. The tannins are supple to the point of creaminess, and the finish warm and spicy. Drink now.
title: 2 Up 2014 Shiraz (South Australia)

description: Fresh, cutting cassis and cranberry acidity lend vitality to this bright, perky Spätburgunder. The palate boasts crisp, primary black chery and berry flavors marked by a pleasant touch of nuttiness. Fine-grained tannins are easy and approachable on the finish. Drink now through 2021.
title: Castell 2014 Casteller Reitsteig Spätburgunder (Franken)

description: Aged in a majority of French oak, and sourced from several vineyards, including many estate, this 100% varietal wine displays a subtle nuance of smoky leather, berry bramble and soft, integrated oak. It's rich, yet balanced with a peppery finish.
title: Mauritson 2014 Zinfandel (Dry Creek Valley)

description: The aromas of jasmine, apricot and bountiful white flowers are exuberant. It's richly sweet with honeyed flavors while maintaining a lovely sense of balance.
designation: Vin de Glacièr Made with Organic Grapes
title: Pacific Rim 2014 Vin de Glacièr Made with Organic Grapes Riesling (Horse Heaven Hills)

description: High and tight aromas of chopped grass and cut tennis ball are met with a pretty floral element that veers toward fresh purple flowers. The family-owned estate, which boasts some of the more dramatic vineyard blocks in the region, offers lemongrass, squeezed lime, citrus zest and Granny Smith apple skins on the palate.
title: Grassini 2014 Estate Grown Estate Bottled Sauvignon Blanc (Happy Canyon of Santa Barbara)

description: French oak-aging has resulted in a wine that's both smooth and fruity. With summer blossom aromas, attractive acidity and a toasty edge, it's stylish, elegant and ready to drink now.
title: Companhia das Quintas 2014 Quinta do Cardo Reserva Branco Siria (Beira Interior)

description: A bit cloudy and still tasting as if it came right out of the new French barrels in which it was fermented, this offers a rich and diverse texture and highlights of lemon oil, beeswax and Aplets & Cotlets. There's a lot going on here, in a blocky chunky, fruity style.
title: Minimus 2014 Dijon Free Chardonnay (Willamette Valley)

description: Some wood aging gives this wine its rich character. The tannins, black-currant fruits and good proportion (36%) of Cabernet Sauvignon in the blend all contribute to the wine's structure and ripeness. Drink from 2018.
title: Château le Calvaire 2014  Bordeaux Supérieur

description: Full-bodied, plush and ripe, this is a bit of a throwback style of Chardonnay, marrying peach, vanilla and mango notes into a delectable whole. Toasted oak gives a bit of structure without straitjacketing the luscious fruit. Drink now–2018.
title: Kim Crawford 2014 Small Parcels Wild Grace Chardonnay (Hawke's Bay)

description: There's an exotic saltiness and briny ocean element to this memorable wine, grandly textured and integrated in terms of oak. Medium in weight, it finds balance between savory, spicy and salty tones and a crispness of Gravenstein apple.
title: The Calling 2014 Jewell Vineyard Dutton Ranch Chardonnay (Russian River Valley)

description: Aromas of white peach and honeysuckle yield to flavors of grapefruit, peach, jasmine and orange blossom. This wine is smooth in the mouth and the zesty finish is characterized by lingering floral flavors.
title: Ouled Thaleb 2014 Sauvignon Blanc (Zenata)

description: Lovely scents of orchard fruit and apple blossom take center stage. The vibrant, savory palate offers crunchy rennet apple, tangerine and mineral while zesty acidity leads to the tangy finish.
title: Caldaro 2014 Söll Pinot Grigio (Alto Adige)

description: Composed berry aromas meet expectations for Tinto Fino from Ribera del Duero. A stout, tannic palate is firm but not too hard or impermeable. Flavors of blackberry and cherry come with dry, woody accents, while peppery blackness rises up on a drawing finish.
title: Aderezo 2014  Ribera del Duero

description: This wine has aromas of pomegranate juice and dried cherry. Flavors suggest pomegranate juice and tart cherry conserves.
title: Côtnar Hills 2014 Merlot (Ukraine)

description: This ripe, rich and soft wine has a good balance between its apple fruit flavors and crisper lemon and gooseberry. The definite tang to the aftertaste gives a great lift. Drink from 2017.
title: Roger et Didier Raimbault 2014  Sancerre

description: Breathtaking scents of white peach and blossom introduce this pristinely peachy wine. It's invigoratingly fresh, laced by a gossamer fringe of sweetness and vivacious lemon-lime acidity that lingers on and on. Delicately textured yet deeply penetrating.
title: Schloss Vollrads 2014 Spätlese Riesling (Rheingau)

description: Whiffs of sea breeze led a saline tone to fresh apple and pear flavors in this brisk, light-bodied wine. It's crisp and approachable, finishing on slightly dilly, herbal note.
title: Martha Clara 2014 Chardonnay (North Fork of Long Island)

description: The medium-sweet stone-fruit and orange-peel flavors are direct and bring appeal. It goes down easy.
title: Maryhill 2014 Riesling (Columbia Valley (WA))

description: This is a layered and complex estate-driven wine. Its firm, sizable tannin foundation promises longevity and enviable structure, while allowing for a plush, lush exuberance of cherry clafoutis, cardamom and salted caramel. Enjoy best 2022 through 2029.
title: Reuling Vineyard 2014 Reuling Vineyard Pinot Noir (Sonoma Coast)

description: Iconic Sauvignon Blanc notes of pink grapefruit, gooseberry, mango and papaya shine brightly on the nose and palate. Ample weight, with balanced acidity and crisp, clean flavors mark this well-made wine at a bargain price.
title: Oliver 2014 Sauvignon Blanc (America)

description: While bolder in body and a more brambly and earthy than the 2015, Grafen Neipperg's Trollinger is attractive for its crisp sour cherry and plum flavors. Brisk acidity and soft, feathery tannins linger nicely on the finish. Drink now.
title: Grafen Neipperg 2014 Halbtrocken Trollinger (Württemberg)

description: This wine from the most northerly of the Côte d'Or appellations is a blend from multiple growers. It has red cherry and plum fruit with a fresh streak, typical of the vintage. Underlying firm tannins will sustain it as it ages. Drink from 2018.
title: Louis Jadot 2014  Marsannay

description: The latest vintage of this white Pinot Noir is a pretty straw color, lacking the rich rose hue of the 2013, but the flavors remain robust and compelling. You'll find plenty of mixed tree fruits, along with suggestions of grass, herb, cracker and toast, with a youthfully yeasty aftertaste.
title: Ghost Hill 2014 Bayliss-Bower Vineyard Pinot Noir Blanc Pinot Noir

description: Enticing floral aromas of elderflower, acacia and a whiff of stone fruit lead the nose. The radiant palate offers juicy tangerine, white peach and a touch of nutmeg alongside zesty acidity.
title: Kettmeir 2014 Müller-Thurgau (Alto Adige)

description: Pear, honeydew melon and floral note lift from the glass. The bright, no-frills palate offers up crisp apple and citrus peel while lively acidity provides a tangy finish.
title: Kupel Wieser 2014 Alfio Moriconi Selection Pinot Grigio (Alto Adige)

description: This offers subdued aromas of elderflower, sage and a slight whiff of tomato vine while the crisp palate shows notes of mint and tropical fruit. Vibrant acidity gives it a clean, racy finish.
title: Poggiobello 2014 Sauvignon (Friuli Colli Orientali)

description: Klipsun Vineyard provides the fruit for this wine, with 10% Boushey Syrah rounding it out. This wine offers aromas of herb and wet copper penny that lead to full-bodied, boisterous fruit flavors. It the aromas come together it could move up a notch or two.
title: Sparkman 2014 Preposterous Malbec (Red Mountain)

description: This is a rich wine, full of rounded black fruits and soft tannins. It is a young style of wine with no wood aging, just the ripe fruits. Drink this wine now.
title: Casa Santa Vitória 2014 Versátil Tinto Red (Alentejano)

description: This is soft and juicy with just a hint of a more tannic structure in the background. It is designed as an immediate easy-drinking wine, full of fruit and blackberry fruits.
title: DFJ Vinhos 2014 Casa do Lago Touriga Nacional-Cabernet Sauvignon (Lisboa)

description: Initially savory tones of bramble and dried herb dissipate to reveal delicate floral and raspberry aromas on this refreshing Pinot Noir rosé. Dry in style with crisp, cutting acidity, it's a versatile and thirst quenching summer quaffer.
title: Fox Run 2014 Rosé of Pinot Noir (Finger Lakes)

description: There is an unusually high (40%) proportion of Sémillon in the blend, which offers the possibility of aging. Although this wine is fresh, lemony and tightly herbaceous now, it will become more rich and the flavors will deepen. Drink from the end of 2016.
title: Château les Pasquets 2014  Entre-Deux-Mers

description: While there's plenty of peachy, primary perfume, crushed slate and stone form the backbone of this deeply mineral Riesling. Dry in style, it's plush, creamy palate is balanced by a bitter edge of lime pith and savory pollen notes. It's a complex wine that should meld and intensify further over the next five years.
title: Wittmann 2014 Westhofen Aulerde Trocken GG Riesling (Rheinhessen)

description: Winemaker Marie-Eve Gilla has a knack for making quality Chardonnay. This one brings aromas of crème fraîche along with pineapple and other tropical fruits. The palate shows restraint.
title: Forgeron 2014 Chardonnay (Columbia Valley (WA))

description: This wine has aromas of cranberry and black raspberry. It offers soft tannins and flavors of cassis, black cherry and cherry vanilla before the fruit-filled finish.
title: Bostavan 2014 Via Etulia Reserve Cabernet Sauvignon (Moldova)

description: This wine is lighter in color, with aromas of sawdust, cherry and barnyard. The style is quite faint, with spice flavors on the finish.
title: Ribbon Cliff 2014 Sangiovese (Columbia Valley (WA))

description: This distinctive wine has a slightly peachy color, which is natural for this varietal. Aromas suggest white pepper and orange zest, while flavors recall peach skin and apricot. A nervy, crisp balance makes it feel lively and refreshing on the tongue, despite full body.
title: Boeger 2014 Pinot Gris (El Dorado)

description: Good depth of flavor and a nicely tannic texture make this hearty wine interesting and appetizing. Earth, oak and chocolate waft from the glass and turn to substantive red cherry and red-currant flavors on the palate, while firm tannins and good acidity give grip to the mouthfeel.
title: Naggiar 2014 Estate Sangiovese (Sierra Foothills)

description: This limited-production wine shows great structure appropriate for a wine that can age. It has a solid texture, full of rich cherry and black-plum fruits and a fresh line of acidity. The tannins give shape to the wine, which will certainly age. Drink from 2017.
title: Domaine Pral 2014 Les Charmes  (Morgon)

description: This opens with subdued aromas reminiscent of acacia flower, toasted nut and citrus drop while the lean, zesty palate suggests lemon rind, yellow apple skin and bitter almond. Bracing acidity provides a brisk framework.
title: Monte Tondo 2014 Casette Foscarin  (Soave Classico)

description: This wine is mostly Roussanne (58%) with the rest equal parts Marsanne and Grenache Blanc. The aromas of herb and peach are light and are followed by elegant lightly styled fruit flavors.
title: Sparkman 2014 Apparition White Wine White (Yakima Valley)

description: Brawny tannins meet mild acidity in this powerful, rich wine that's full-bodied and decadent. Chocolate, mocha and caramel notes are etched into its soft, rounded layers of generous red fruit.
title: Caymus 2014 Special Selection Cabernet Sauvignon (Napa Valley)

description: This is sourced from old vines, and brings typically intense, phenolic aromatics in a dry, extended and lingering style. Citrus-rind scents and dried-apple flavors abound, and it's textural and persistent, though not what you'd call fruity. The length and development are its best attributes.
title: Amity 2014 Riesling (Willamette Valley)

description: Lush and bright, this comes from one of the coolest sites sourced by the producer, high up and facing the Pacific Ocean. A gravelly texture holds on tightly along the palate, as pear, peach and baked pineapple vie for attention.
title: Ramey 2014 Platt Vineyard Chardonnay (Sonoma Coast)

description: Mandarin-orange zest, cut Asian pear, lemon blossom and slight beeswax show on the fresh nose of this bottling by young winemaker Mike Callahan. It's extremely dry on the palate, like licking limestone, with hints of nectarine blossom and lemon-skin flavor.
title: MaidenStoen 2014 Zabala Vineyard Riesling (Monterey County)

description: With spice and layers of wood aging over the black-cherry fruit, this is a wine in progress. It has the bright character of the vintage, full of acidity and with the potential of great fruitiness. From the Vergennes sub-section of the Corton Grand Cru, it has a good future. Drink from 2021.
title: Jean-Luc and Paul Aegerter 2014  Corton Vergennes

description: This biodynamically farmed vineyard is often found as a named designate, and for good reason. It's one of the Willamette Valley's most distinctive and evocative sites, with less of an emphasis on pure fruit flavors, and more attention to details such as herbal tea, earth, stone and dried leaf. The fruit is tart and lightly herbal, like a fresh-picked mountain berry.
title: St. Innocent 2014 Momtazi Vineyard Pinot Noir (McMinnville)

description: This wine brings enchanting aromas of toast, spice and cardamom that lead to reserved, exquisitely balanced fruit flavors. It shows a lot of restraint and delicacy.
title: Rasa 2014 Dream Deferred Wallula Vineyard Chardonnay (Horse Heaven Hills)
